anatomy.description: Dark pill-shaped button (303×56px, border-radius 120px). Background: --button/default/bg-default (#1d1d23). Acts as the base surface for the countdown visualization.

Absolutely positioned dark layer over the container. Grows from left to right as the countdown progresses. Uses mix-blend-multiply to progressively darken the button surface. Width represents elapsed time (0px at start, 303px at expiry).
Text label centered over the button. 'Accept' in the Driver App context. Uses mix-blend-difference to remain readable over both the unfilled and filled areas at all animation positions.

states.name: full
states.description: Timer just started. Fill overlay width: 0px. Button appears at normal dark background. Full time remaining.

states.name: middle
states.description: Timer approximately halfway through. Fill overlay width: ~147px (~48.5% of button width). Left half of button is darkened.

states.name: state3
states.description: Timer nearly expired. Fill overlay width: 303px (full button width). Entire button is darkened by the overlay.

stateNote: Three visual states defined in Figma, representing snapshots of a continuous countdown animation. No hover, pressed, disabled, or focus states defined.
variantNote: No type variants. Single visual style.
sizeNote: Single size only. No size variants.

fillOverlay.colorToken: TODO: Confirm design token for fill overlay background color.

typography.note: Uses Google Sans Flex Regular (not SemiBold). mix-blend-difference ensures readability over both unfilled and filled button areas.

usageContext: Timed action prompts where the user must act within a limited countdown window.
Confirmed from Driver App: 'Finding a trip → New trip flow' (node 2007:8976). Shown when a new trip is offered to the driver.
Single instance per screen — not for repeated or parallel use.
notes: No type variants — single visual style.
Three states in Figma: full (0% fill), middle (~48.5% fill), state3 (100% fill). These are animation snapshots, not discrete interactive states.
The fill overlay uses mix-blend-multiply. The label uses mix-blend-difference. Both are confirmed and load-bearing.
Font weight is Regular (400), not SemiBold. Confirmed from Figma.
